task: I want to put together a really simple at-home yoga plan using only free YouTube videos because I’m trying to ease into a routine without paying for an app. Could you start on Google and find at least three yoga instructors who seem especially approachable, beginner-friendly, or a little funny and who clearly post free classes on YouTube, so I have a shortlist of personalities that feel welcoming rather than intimidating. For each one, grab the instructor name, the YouTube channel name, and one example video title. Then head over to YouTube and, using that shortlist, pick one morning yoga video that’s around 20 minutes long from one of those instructors so I have an easy option for weekdays; please note the exact title, duration, and link. After that, find a separate Vinyasa flow video on YouTube that’s about 30 minutes long so I have a slightly longer practice option too, and give me its title and link as well. Please open the two chosen videos in separate tabs so I can compare them, and start playing the 30-minute Vinyasa one long enough to confirm it’s the right video before leaving that tab open. In the end, send me the full yoga plan with the instructor shortlist plus both selected videos and their details.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent provided a shortlist of three yoga instructors (Adriene Mishler, Kassandra Reinhardt, and Charlie Follows). For each instructor, it included the instructor's name, the YouTube channel name, and an example video title. This information was gathered through Google searches and YouTube metadata verification, satisfying the requirement.

R2: success - The agent successfully identified a morning yoga video ("Morning Yoga Flow | 20-Minute Morning Yoga Practice") from the instructor "Yoga With Adriene" (who was included in the shortlist). The agent provided the title, duration (21:47), and the link (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LqXZ628YNj4) in the final response. This satisfies the requirement for R2.

R3: success - The agent successfully identified a Vinyasa flow video ("30 Min Vinyasa Yoga Flow | Full Body Yoga for All Levels" by Charlie Follows), verified its duration (1818 seconds, which is approximately 30 minutes), and included the title and link in the final response.

R4: success - The agent successfully opened both the morning yoga video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LqXZ628YNj4) and the Vinyasa flow video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HbQfzWDZ2M4) in separate tabs. In step 15, the agent explicitly confirmed that the Vinyasa video was playing (`"paused": false`) and had reached a `currentTime` of approximately 27.5 seconds, satisfying the requirement to start playing the video briefly.

R5: success - The agent provided a comprehensive final response that includes the instructor shortlist (with names, channel names, and example videos), the chosen morning yoga video (with title, duration, and link), and the chosen Vinyasa flow video (with title and link). All these components are presented in a single, coherent plan as requested.
